flashcards.back: A cryptographic system that uses pairs of keys: public keys (which may be disseminated widely) and private keys (which are known only to the owner). This system enables secure communication and digital signatures.

flashcards.front: Why must private keys be kept secret?
flashcards.back: Private keys provide complete control over associated blockchain assets. If compromised, an attacker can transfer all funds without any recovery options in decentralized systems.

flashcards.front: How is a public key derived from a private key?
flashcards.back: Through one-way mathematical functions (like elliptic curve multiplication in Bitcoin) that are computationally easy to perform in one direction but practically impossible to reverse.

flashcards.front: What is the role of a digital signature in blockchain?
flashcards.back: A digital signature proves that a transaction came from the owner of the funds without revealing the private key. It can be verified by anyone using the corresponding public key.

flashcards.front: What analogy best describes public and private keys?
flashcards.back: A private key is like a master key to a safety deposit box, while the public key is like the publicly listed box number. Anyone can send items to the box number, but only the master key can open it.
